title: cochlearia officinalis subsp. groenlandica
intro: Cochlearia officinalis subsp. groenlandica, also known as Arctic cress or Greenland cress, is a subspecies of the plant Cochlearia officinalis, which is a type of succulent herb native to the Arctic tundra regions. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, including its ability to act as a diuretic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic, making it useful in treating a variety of health issues such as urinary tract infections and skin conditions. The health benefits of Arctic cress include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve circulation, and boost the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy for colds and respiratory issues. In terms of herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of Arctic cress can be used to make teas, infusions, and salves, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
